titleStmt.title: Letter from Maria Edgeworth to Frances Anne Edgeworth, January 23, 1844
titleStmt.author: Maria Edgeworth
titleStmt.sponsor: Wake Forest University, University of Tennessee, Xavier University, Texas A&M University
titleStmt.principal: Susan Egenolf, Meredith Hale, Hilary Havens, Jessica Richard, and Robin Runia

editionStmt.edition: First digital edition in TEI, date:

availability.licence: Distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) Unported License
availability.p: Reproduced by courtesy of Bodleian Library
availability.licence: Distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0) Unported License

msDesc.head:
Letter from
Maria Edgeworth 1768-1849 71477273 62567029
to Frances Anne Edgeworth,

editorialDecl.p: Edgeworth’s spelling and punctuation are retained in transcription, including non-standard spelling, line breaks, and hyphenation of words. The long s and ligatured forms are silently emended.

div.p:
93
With respect to my rose
trellis -- there is no rose
without thorns ! --
Il n'y a ici qu'une petite
difficulle - manque de
fonds --

If you can afford to advance
three pounds for me till
I return home well & good
I shall be glad to have the
new wire and new post
trellis - and moreover
I shall be obliged to you ma'am

But if you cannot with-
out inconvenience do this
I would rather let the con-
cern [underline]wholly[/underline] or brokenly quite
alone till I return, when in
the course of time & [unclear] sucking my
paws[/unclear] at economic home I shall
be able to pay for [underline]all[/underline] - includ
ing L50 which I have borrowed
& must pay in May to the good
[unclear][/unclear]
2) Every guinea shilling or
penny that I can rap or
run I shall want to get
get me over Easter, whether
in London most expensive
or on the road almost
equally expensive visiting
friends & bails to servants
vous comprenez [unclear][/unclear] or in
M[superscript]rs[/superscript]. -'s phrase " Do I make my=
self understood "

I know you will at
this moment or perhaps
before this moment say to
yourself that on or about
Feb[superscript]y[/superscript]. 9 - or 10t[superscript]h[/superscript]. Maria will
have Quarter Ann[superscript]y[/superscript]. (due Jan[superscript]y[/superscript]-) £85,,10,,0
[insertion]out of[/insertion] which surely she can afford
this poor little £3 for her rose trellis

But dear madam please
comprehend that the above named
£85,,10 - plus £21,,10- must go to
your Ladyship for House acct
On the first of March next - And
if I touch or devour any part of it
(3 now I cannot replace it then
without pinching difficulty
to my clearing out of London -

I hate pinching difficulties &
always avoid them to the best of
my capacity & discretion

Nota bene I am indulging
myself in setting up 35 West Indian
beautiful birds (just received
from Mr[superscript]s[/superscript]. Carmichael) and which
I have destined for a recess in
Fannys drawing room where they
will look beautiful & she likes the
thought - The mere setting up will
cost £5 - the glass and frame
work habitation for the beauties
will come to about £5.10 -
Fanny insists upon paying for
the house - I for the birds -
" Each pleasure has its price " &c

[deletion]I have had[/deletion] My old embroider
ed skreens or rather my dear
mother [underline]your [/underline]old embroidered
skreens which I gave you & took
back from you [unclear][/unclear] gold ring
fashion I have had elegantly
mounted by Law, with gilt
framer & plate glass and
[unclear][/unclear]
We go to Lady Raffles on Friday
morning - return same day -
Tea at Lady Dinman's on
Saturday to meet my Lord whom
I much like or admire - else I
would not stir a foot for him and
would rather take tea at home

Sunday to Capt[superscript]n[/superscript]. Griffths Lestocks
friend - and the friend of Order
wrote the [unclear]mel[/unclear][superscript]g[/superscript]. regulation book you know
or if you don't [unclear]vide [/unclear]Hall [unclear]closet[/unclear]
if you care to know -

He has been very kind to me & I know that
15. [insertion]Instant [/insertion]Monday - I believe I shall dine
for the last time - with Honora & Capt B
and see the baby & Sophy whom
we have never yet seen -

They have very judiciously kept,
her perfectly quiet - Oh how wish
but malheur passé - &c -

I cannot yet positively say on which
day tuesday wednesday thursday
we go to the Moilliets but will
write again when quite near my
projection

Mean time Dearest
mother your poor poor little pencil
commission shall be cared for
and is that all! you can think of
for me to do for you my dearest
madam -

I do not know when FME
wrote to you last or whether she told
you our history up to the day we
came here - But for fear of missing
so important an event I must (go to page
second
